996.name: IMS Bearing Failure
996.description: The intermediate shaft bearing can fail catastrophically, sending metal debris throughout the engine. Single-row bearings (2000-2005) are most at risk.
996.prevention: Replace with IMS Solution ceramic bearing or LN Engineering retrofit. Use quality oil (Motul, LiquiMoly) and change every 5k miles.
996.fix: IMS bearing replacement (engine-out recommended). If bearing has failed and damaged engine, full rebuild $15-25K.

996.name: Bore Scoring
996.description: Cylinders develop scoring/gouging on cylinder walls, typically cylinders 1 and 6. Caused by localized overheating from insufficient oil spray or debris.
996.prevention: Short oil change intervals, proper warm-up, LN Engineering cylinder coating if doing preventive rebuild.
996.fix: Engine rebuild with Nikasil or LN Engineering coated cylinders. Or engine replacement.

996.name: Rear Main Seal (RMS) Leak
996.description: The rear main seal between engine and transmission leaks oil. Common on all M96/M97 engines.
symptoms: Oil drip from bell housing area
996.fix: Replace RMS. Requires transmission removal. Often done with IMS bearing and clutch at same time.

996.name: Coolant Pipes/Fittings
996.description: Plastic coolant distribution pipes and fittings crack with age and heat cycling.
996.prevention: Replace plastic fittings with aluminum upgrades from FCP Euro or Pelican Parts.
996.fix: Replace cracked pipe/fitting. Upgrade to aluminum.

997.name: IMS Bearing Failure
997.description: Same as 996 but 997.1 uses single-row bearing which is actually MORE failure-prone. 997.2 (2009+) eliminated the IMS bearing entirely.
997.fix: Same as 996. 997.2 does NOT have this issue.

997.name: Bore Scoring
997.description: Same issue as 996, affecting M97 engines. 3.8L engines less prone than 3.6L.
997.prevention: Short oil change intervals. Proper warm-up.

997.name: AOS (Air-Oil Separator) Failure
997.description: The air-oil separator diaphragm fails, causing oil to be ingested into the intake manifold.
997.fix: Replace AOS unit. Best done with new vacuum lines.

997.name: Turbo Wastegate Rattle
997.description: 997.2 Turbo wastegate actuator arm develops play, causing a rattling noise on deceleration.
997.fix: Replace wastegate actuator arm or upgrade to TiAL wastegate.

993.name: D-Chunk Cylinder Liner Failure
993.description: The Alusil cylinder liners can develop 'D-shaped' chunks that break away from the cylinder wall. More common on Varioram engines.
symptoms: Catastrophic engine failure in severe cases
993.prevention: Proper warm-up, quality oil, avoid sustained high RPM when cold.
993.fix: Engine rebuild with Nikasil or aftermarket sleeves.

993.name: Chain Tensioner Failure
993.description: The hydraulic chain tensioners can fail, allowing timing chains to become loose. Updated tensioners (yellow/green) solve this.
993.prevention: Replace with updated tensioner pads and tensioners.
993.fix: Replace chain tensioners and guides. Check chain stretch.

993.name: Oil Leaks (General)
993.description: Air-cooled engines leak oil. The 993 is no exception. Common from cam covers, chain housing, and case halves.
993.fix: Reseal as needed. Case half reseal requires engine removal.

964.name: Dual Mass Flywheel Failure
964.description: The DMF can fail, especially on cars with aggressive driving. Heavy clutch pedal and chatter are signs.
964.prevention: Smooth clutch engagement. Replace with single-mass flywheel conversion.
964.fix: Replace DMF or convert to single-mass flywheel (with appropriate clutch).

964.name: Oil Leaks
964.description: Cam cover, chain tensioner covers, and case half gaskets are common oil leak sources.

964.name: Distributor Drive Belt
964.description: The rubber belt that drives the distributor can crack and fail, causing the engine to lose spark.

991.name: Connecting Rod Recall (Early GT3)
991.description: Early 991.1 GT3 engines had connecting rod bolts that could loosen, causing catastrophic engine failure. Porsche issued recall.
991.prevention: Verify recall was completed. Check VIN with Porsche.
991.fix: Porsche replaced engines under recall. Free.

991.name: PDK Mechatronic Unit Issues
991.description: Some PDK transmissions develop issues with the mechatronic valve body.
991.prevention: Regular PDK fluid changes every 40K miles.

992.name: Wrist Pin Noise (GT3)
992.description: Some early 992 GT3s exhibit wrist pin noise, particularly on cold starts.
992.fix: Porsche has addressed under warranty for affected cars.

cayenne_955.name: Coolant Pipe/Tube Failure
cayenne_955.description: Plastic coolant distribution pipes behind the engine crack and leak. Can cause catastrophic overheating if not caught.
cayenne_955.prevention: Replace plastic pipes with aluminum upgrades (Rennline, ECS).
cayenne_955.fix: Replace coolant pipes. Upgrade to aluminum.

cayenne_955.name: Transfer Case Failure
cayenne_955.description: The transfer case can develop leaks and bearing noise.

cayenne_955.name: Air Suspension Failure
cayenne_955.description: Air springs and compressor wear out.
cayenne_955.prevention: Avoid overloading. Keep air lines clean.
cayenne_955.fix: Replace air springs and/or compressor. Coilover conversion available.

986_boxster.name: IMS Bearing Failure
986_boxster.description: Same M96 engine IMS bearing issue as 996.

986_boxster.name: RMS Oil Leak
986_boxster.description: Rear main seal leak, same as 996.

986_boxster.name: Convertible Top Issues
986_boxster.description: Hydraulic top mechanisms can develop leaks and motor failures.
986_boxster.prevention: Keep hydraulic fluid topped up. Lubricate seals.
986_boxster.fix: Rebuild hydraulic cylinders or replace motor.

taycan.name: 12V Battery Drain
taycan.description: The 12V auxiliary battery drains quickly when the car sits, especially with connected services active.
taycan.prevention: Keep plugged in. Disable unnecessary connected services.
taycan.fix: Replace 12V battery. Software update may help.

taycan.name: Software/Infotainment Glitches
taycan.description: PCM system can freeze, lag, or display errors.
taycan.fix: Software update at dealer. Hard reset (hold power button 10s).
